free_text: Pile neutron absorption cross sections of some of the
elements
Pile oscillator, graphite low
energy experimental pile.
.Maxwell spectrum
.Natural elements, as pure as possible.
.Pile oscillator, method described by A.M.Weinberg
and H.C.Schweinler, Phys.Rev,74,851(1948). During
oscillation the sample remains 20 seconds in the
centre of the pile, and then 20 seconds at the edge.
.No details given.
From: M.Ross, J.S.Story,1949, Rep.Pros.Phys., 12
(London : Physical Society), p. 291.
.Slowing down and scattering of the neutrons by the
sample were established to be negligible.
Data taken from Proc.Phys.Soc.UK,A63,1175,1950
.Quoted error contains error of the
measurement.
G.C.
Hh
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrected according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
BIB update. FLAG deleted (not used). Subent 42 added.
No self-screening correction, and either a complete
chemical analysis was made on the sample or the
element was specially prepared in a pure state.
Self-screening correction was applied and no chemical
analysis was done on the sample. Therefore the data
may be subject to systematic error, due to impurities
with a high cross section.

activity of the polonium produced.
